extra.reason: Monorepo tree, go.mod/go.work, README/LICENSE/SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING, .gitignore. Verified: go build, go vet, gofmt clean.
package.json + pnpm-workspace.yaml + per-package stubs (11 packages), Makefile targets. Verified: pnpm install, make build/test/lint/bootstrap all pass.

8 canonical protocol schemas (adapter, event, workflow, knowledge, task, evidence, approval, flow) written to protocol/*.schema.json, draft 2020-12, grounded in plan §5.4, §7, §9-10, §12.9, §16-19. Validated: valid JSON, ajv-cli schema compile clean (date-time format warnings are ajv-cli plugin config only, not schema defects).

Created docs/adapters/manifest-format.md documenting every adapter manifest field (id, name, version, protocol, runtime, provides, permissions.network/filesystem/secrets, operations.<name>.side_effect/approval) and how the Go core supervisor uses the manifest across the §5.2 lifecycle (start -> initialize -> validate manifest -> grant capabilities -> execute -> receive result -> record evidence -> stop/reuse), referencing protocol/adapter.schema.json as canonical. Created examples/atlassian-playwright/adapter-manifest.example.yaml, the verbatim §5.4 Atlassian Cloud manifest. Validated with a throwaway node script (js-yaml + ajv/dist/2020 + ajv-formats installed via npm install --no-save in scratch dir): YAML parses cleanly and passes ajv.compile(schema)/validate(doc) against protocol/adapter.schema.json with zero errors. No protocol/*.schema.json files modified.

Wrote docs/security/threat-model.md organizing §15/§16/§24 into assets, trust boundaries (Go core trusted / TS adapters semi-trusted+sandboxed / external content untrusted), 7 threat/mitigation subsections, and residual-risk section flagging M9 items (adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool checksums, recovery) as not yet implemented.

Created ADR-0001 through ADR-0015 under docs/architecture/ transcribing all 15 decisions from plan §25, plus a README index; each ADR follows the Status/Context/Decision/Consequences structure grounded in the plan's surrounding sections.

Go codegen: go-jsonschema as go.mod tool dep + //go:generate directive in pkg/protocol/doc.go -> types_generated.go (44 types). TS codegen: json-schema-to-typescript + json-schema-to-zod in packages/schema-types/scripts/generate.mjs -> src/generated/*.ts + *.zod.ts (interfaces + Zod validators per §5.5), tsconfig+build/typecheck wired. Makefile generate/protocol-check targets wired (protocol-check re-generates and git-diffs to catch staleness). Verified: go build/vet, tsc --noEmit, make generate/build/lint/test all pass. Note: json-schema-to-zod (StefanTerdell) is dev-tooling-only (no runtime exposure) but flagged unmaintained as of its own README; fine for now, revisit if it breaks.

Newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. TS side: packages/adapter-sdk/src/stdio.ts (serveStdio helper) + prototypeAdapter.ts (initialize validates against generated AdapterManifestSchema, execute/sleep supports cancellation, shutdown exits). Go side: internal/adapters/stdio.go (Client: Start/Call/Shutdown/Kill, ctx-based cancellation sends a cancel notification). Integration test test/integration/adapter_stdio_test.go (build-tag integration): TestInitializeHandshake proves Go can start+handshake a TS adapter with schema validation; TestExecuteCancellation proves a 150ms ctx timeout aborts a 5s sleep op promptly. Verified: make lint/test/integration-test all pass. Satisfies M0 acceptance criteria on adapter start + cancellation/timeout.

.github/workflows/ci.yml: checkout, setup-go+setup-node/pnpm, then make bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test on push+PR. Scoped to what M0 actually built; broader Section 21.1 items (license scan, secret scan, multi-platform smoke build, contract tests) are later-milestone concerns (M9 hardening, adapter contract tests). Verified: workflow YAML parses correctly, and every make target it calls (bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test) passes locally.

All 9 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified: Go starts a TS adapter (stdio prototype), both sides validate the same schemas (generated Go structs + Zod validators, manifest validated in TestInitializeHandshake), cancellation/timeout work (TestExecuteCancellation), CI detects stale generated code (protocol-check in ci.yml).
